name: Room in a 5 Bedroom Apartment, Charles Street Great, Mountjoy

reviews.author: Alix LOPEZ
reviews.text: Living at DCU College Park feels like the student version of a luxury resort… if luxury resorts had random fire alarms and people stealing your milk. The rooms are cozy, the showers are basically a spa with that water pressure, and the kitchens are where strangers become friends (or at least cooking buddies). You’re ri

reviews.author: Xiaoyang Li
reviews.text: The room is in a very poor condition. Quite small with a shower head that barely working. When we arrived, they informed us that the room is not ready yet, so we had to wait for about 20 mins, and then the room was apparently still not ready. Eventually, it turns out to be a broken sink which they fixed later that day.

reviews.author: Chinmaya Kaundanya
reviews.text: This is my third year at DCU Rooms, and it has always been a pleasant experience. As an international student, I was initially anxious about how things would turn out—right from my flatmates to my studies and even fun events. But DCU Rooms made the journey easy. Everything, from labs to stores to cafés, is close by and

reviews.author: Habeeb Balogun
reviews.text: I've had a great experience with this accommodation so far. The team has been incredibly responsive and helpful throughout the move-in period. Every query has been handled promptly and professionally, which has made settling in much easier. A special mention to Francisco Luiza, his willingness to assist have really sto

reviews.author: Asya Konaklıoğlu
reviews.text: I love my room. I am staying here for 4 months, I loved everything. My room is big, they are organising lots of events, so close to the city center. There a lots of places that you can eat at the point. Also you have dunnes store and boots near you. You can always get your parcel from reception, it is 24/7

reviews.author: Fox 42
reviews.text: In general a nice, modern student accomodation. Tooms include a desk, bed, a small bathroom and a good amount of storage space. The shared kitchen offer wn acceptable level of facilities including cpiches, ovens and furnace. While I'd love to give a higher rating, the organisational aspects prevent me from this Whrn mo

reviews.text: Very convenient location of the hotel. Bus stop nearby. Easy to get from the airport and anywhere in Dublin. 10 minutes walk to the centre.When we checked in, we were given a tiny and not very clean room. But the reception fixed everything and gave us another room.You can also get a hairdryer, soap, shampoo, toothbrush

reviews.author: fion yu
reviews.text: I forgot my key in the room. They helped me solve the problem very quickly. Very good!

reviews.author: Nicole Claeys
reviews.text: Great building with everything you need. Perfect location, cozy beds for two, private bathroom, an amazing shared kitchen, and incredible common areas!!

reviews.author: Katana Hill
reviews.text: Lovely place to stay, fair price especially being so close to the city centre. Communal areas were clean, staff friendly, plenty of lovely food choices around the area, overall no complaints and would recommend to anyone wanting to explore the beautiful city of Dublin!

reviews.author: Melisa Solon
reviews.text: A blonde woman, her arm covered in tattoos and with a Portuguese accent, was incredibly rude when we went to ask her to show us a room we'd already visited three times. I asked for photos, and she responded so rudely. My friend, who's been twice, told me she'd been treated the same way. She's so ill-mannered and doesn'

reviews.author: Emma Migliorini
reviews.text: Let’s start with the positives: free water fountain, nice outdoor garden area with ping pong tables, and friendly staff. The location is good, you can reach the city center in less than 10 minutes. Rooms: small but functional. There’s a queen-size bed whether you book a single or a double room, but it might be too smal

name: Yugo Ardcairn House
address: Arran Quay, Dublin, D07 W5F3, Ireland

reviews.author: Kenia Jimenez
reviews.text: Facilities are really nice and always clean. But the best thing is the staff, especially the morning team. They are very friendly and always willing to help.Yoni especially, he was always smiling and helping. Made my stay very nice! I recommend Ardcairn House to everyone🥰

reviews.author: Martin
reviews.text: Good student accommodation. Rooms big enough for a person. Own bathroom, which is important. The shared kitchen is also good. All the appliances you need in there. Very near from the city centre. Just 20 minutes walk or a few minutes with the bus, which is only 2 minutes from the building. Laundry on site. If you want

reviews.author: J H (TEA TlME)
reviews.text: This dormitory is really good and really bad at the same time. Let me explain: They have a small gym with the basic equipment, a cinema room, a music room, two tabletennis plates (one in- and one outdoor), two small bowling alleys, good Wifi, a big Laundry room (so you never have to wait for one to be free), sometimes

name: Room in a 6 Bedroom Apartment, 59 South Circular Road, Portobello, Dublin 8

reviews.author: Adam Bucharaya
reviews.text: My experience at Griffith Halls of Residence was absolutely terrible. The accommodation is extremely basic for the very high price they charge, and whenever something needs to be fixed, it takes ages for them to do it, while in the winter time they shut down the heaters so they can save money, they don’t care about the

reviews.author: Christine BXL
reviews.text: We stayed at Griffith Halls of Residence as a family for two nights and found it a good and practical option in Dublin. The apartment was clean, functional, and well located, with friendly staff and good value for money. There are a few areas that could be improved. The kitchen equipment is very basic – it worked for u

reviews.text: Spacious and well-equipped apartment with a proper double bed and a comfortable sofa in the living room/kitchen. A hairdryer is available at reception. The kitchen was newly equipped, and the smart decision to provide us with exactly 4 plates, 4 glasses, 4 bowls, 4 forks, etc., forced all my travel companions to keep e

reviews.text: Ok for one night or if accommodation elsewhere is very expensive. Fixtures in the room were cheap and nasty and the communal kitchen area was very tired and in need of a good clean. Staff were very nice and late checkout for 20 euro was appreciated too.

reviews.author: Gizem Naz Sezen
reviews.text: I am very disappointed with how my situation was handled by Yugo New Mill. I repeatedly visited reception over a long period of time trying to resolve an accommodation issue that was not my fault. Each time, I explained the situation and asked for help, yet I was never able to speak with anyone in a managerial position

Please make sure to give some courses in mental health for him so he start harming the students living ther

address: Ballymun Rd, Dublin 11, D11 ETF7, Ireland

reviews.text: I want to express my sincere gratitude to the entire accommodation team for their kindness, patience, and support. From the cinema room where I unwind with friends, to the playroom and designated smoking areas, everything here is designed for comfort and convenience. The staff's friendliness makes a big difference, a

reviews.text: I stayed at this property for two weeks during a study holiday. The residence is modern and has every comfort. The ensuite room is quite spacious, although I didn't find it very clean, and the common kitchen is large and has all the appliances. In addition, the property is very close to the supermarket and bus stops, w

reviews.text: Really happy with my experience here so far! The rooms are comfortable, the common areas are always clean, and the overall atmosphere is friendly and welcoming. The rooms are clean and well-maintained, and the common areas are always kept at a high standard of cleanliness. Public transport is very clos

name: Ardee Point
address: Newmarket, The Liberties, Dublin, D08 KPP4, Ireland

reviews.author: vendi sefcic
reviews.text: I had a very positive experience staying at Ardee Student Accommodation. The location is convenient, the rooms are clean and modern, and the staff are friendly and helpful. I would definitely recommend it to students in Dublin.

reviews.author: Oluwatobi Ogunsugba
reviews.text: Ardee point is a great place to live. Location wise, its close enough to many amenities and has great transport links to and beyond the city centre. Some of the amenities like the private dining and cinema have been well put to use by me and my friends to take a break from academic life, The current accommodation team

reviews.author: Henna Udhwani Ganglani
reviews.text: I went to the reception to ask if I could get a tour of the accommodation and they helped me so much even though I didnt have a slot booked! The girls at the desk are kind and attentive all the time.

name: Yugo Lee Point
address: S Main St, Centre, Cork, Ireland

reviews.author: Sibeesh Venu
reviews.text: Our stay here was great. The rooms, lifts, doorways, kitchen were maintained very well. The staffs were very helpful. The room has storage spaces, table and lamp too. When we visited there were constructions happening nearby and as there were many pubs nearby, it is a bit noisy until the midnight. The shower gels, sham

reviews.author: Eleni Mitakidou
reviews.text: + :Nice location,private bathroom,shared kitchen/living room,polite staff,value for money,you could leave luggage before check in and after check out. - :Quite noisy during night(from outside),bed was short(so if you are more than 1,75cm you would have an issue-cause bed was in the corner and wardrobe was next to it, s

reviews.author: Ann Geary
reviews.text: Fabulous location for students at multiple colleges in Cork! Lovely helpful staff. Clean and plenty of storage with a Tesco Express right outside, ideal for groceries.

reviews.author: Alessandra Fenu
reviews.text: The accommodation was comfortable and had everything needed, even though it wasn’t particularly luxurious. The location was excellent and very convenient. The staff were friendly and welcoming. The bathroom was spacious, and the shower was excellent. Unfortunately there was not Aircon and the air in the room was very s

reviews.author: Ruairi Basso
reviews.text: Very good overall experience living at Amnis. Everything is top quality in the bedroom and kitchen. The view onto the lake is amazing. All the receptionists are so nice especially Jack!!

reviews.author: Aashvi Hitesh
reviews.text: Amazing stay! The place is clean, comfortable, and well-maintained. The staff are all very friendly, but Jack truly stands out. He is incredibly kind, always willing to help with anything, and patiently answers every question. He really goes above and beyond to make your stay easy and enjoyable. Highly recommend!

name: Árasáin P&V
address: 9-13 Blackhall Pl, Stoneybatter, Dublin 7, D07 P2NX, Ireland

reviews.author: Blendi Berisha
reviews.text: I stayed here during my studies, and I had a very positive experience there. The staff were always friendly, responsive, and helpful, which made a real difference throughout my stay. A special mention to Dan, who was very friendly. It was reassuring to know that any issues or questions were handled quickly and professi

reviews.author: Natalie
reviews.text: The worst experience ever. Very poorly managed place with no respect for the residents. Messy check in procedure, no live communication- they only send generic messages via Booking. Some people had to stay outside for the whole night because they couldn't get the keys on check in. Poor ventilation and no central air co

reviews.text: I'm having a great experience at this student accommodation. First of all, the location is excellent — it's just a 20-minute walk from the city centre and very well connected to public transport and nearby universities, which has been super convenient. The facilities are always clean and well-maintained. There's also a

reviews.author: L
reviews.text: Pretty good place to stay - very quiet and comfy! The only thing that bothered is that we couldn’t check-in a little bit earlier than 3 PM. I wish there was an option for that because the check out is at 10 AM.. Also enjoyed the window view

reviews.author: Liam R
reviews.text: This student accommodation is terrific! Throughout my stay I was so impressed by the facilities which are all very modern and up to date. reviews.author: Ola Rarity Szczukiewicz

reviews.author: Deepesh Siva
reviews.text: I had a great experience with Highlight Parkgate Student Accommodation. The rooms are clean, modern, and well-maintained, and the common areas are perfect for studying and socialising. The location is very convenient with easy access to public transport, shops, and essential facilities. The management team is friendly,

reviews.author: tanvi shenoy
reviews.text: Amazing student accommodation! They've got great amenities and the staff are super friendly and helpful! Highly recommend this to any incoming students, especially inter national students. The location is perfect to travel easily across Dublin!

reviews.author: Laoise Geraghty
reviews.text: Very happy with this accommodation, everyone is very helpful! It’s in a great location for busses/luas and Heuston station. Great facilities with the gym and study room too.

name: Room in a 7 Bedroom Apartment, 43 Brookhaven Drive, Corduff, Dublin 15

reviews.author: Deepesh Siva
reviews.text: I had a great experience with Highlight Parkgate Student Accommodation. The rooms are clean, modern, and well-maintained, and the common areas are perfect for studying and socialising. The location is very convenient with easy access to public transport, shops, and essential facilities. The management team is friendly,

reviews.author: tanvi shenoy
reviews.text: Amazing student accommodation! They've got great amenities and the staff are super friendly and helpful! Highly recommend this to any incoming students, especially inter national students. The location is perfect to travel easily across Dublin!

reviews.author: Laoise Geraghty
reviews.text: Very happy with this accommodation, everyone is very helpful! It’s in a great location for busses/luas and Heuston station. Great facilities with the gym and study room too.

name: Ashlin House
address: Bandon Rd, The Lough, Cork, T12 FX0D, Ireland

reviews.author: Yue Cao
reviews.text: It is really close to UCC main campus. The whole dormitory environment is very new. Hot water can be enjoyed at any time. The mattress is very large and soft. Sound insulation is also good.

reviews.author: Iyedr
reviews.text: Mixed feelings regarding my stay here. The position of the accommodation is easy to reach from the center even with a brief walk, although staff was unwelcoming at check in. Don’t expect a warm welcome. The room has a decent size and was clean, but the shower has low pressure and the quality of the mattress was so poor

reviews.author: Viviana Pereira
reviews.text: I am writing to formally express my dissatisfaction with the current conditions of the accommodation where I am staying. From the very first day, I noticed that the shared kitchen was completely unequipped — there were no pots, cups, cutlery, or even dishwashing liquid. I reported this immediately and was informed that

name: Highlight Thomas Street
address: The Liberties, Dublin 8, D08 EP30, Ireland

reviews.author: Arman Lebellour
reviews.text: I have been staying here for well over 6 months now. I chose it based on previous google reviews I made, and it's safe to say that I haven't been disappointed. There are several maintenance issues we've encountered as a flat (of 8)(e.g. no water) and normally this would be reason enough to downgrade it to a 4 or 3 cons

reviews.author: Shankar Narain
reviews.text: Highlight Thomas Street offers a fantastic student experience in a prime city-centre location. It’s just minutes’ walk from top universities and colleges, and surrounded by shops, cafés, restaurants and transport links — perfect for busy student life. The building has modern, comfortable rooms with all essential bills

reviews.author: Mirda
reviews.text: Best hostel I have ever stayed in! Great social environment for the solo travelers. Multiple events planned daily. Staff members hang out with the guests and help show them around the city. Made new friends from all over the world. Rooms were fantastic with clean bathrooms and showers. My room (room B on fourth floor)

reviews.author: Floor van Rutten
reviews.text: Checking in was easy. We had some problems with the shower but they fixed it the same day. The bed was comfortable and big enough for me and my partner. reviews.author: Ishaan Aryan
reviews.text: I lived at Yugo Broadstone for almost two years and it was a genuinely great experience, one I'd recommend without hesitation. reviews.author: Matthew Hartford
reviews.text: nice student accomodation. tenants are pleasent and nice staff. it is a more quiet yugo accomodation compared to other sites in dublin. only downside would be: it is a peripheral attachment of the larger dominick so the reception can be less available in person.

reviews.author: Harshith S

Moving away from home can be challenging, but the welcoming and supportive environment here made the transition much easier. reviews.author: Sean Price
reviews.text: A homeless man entered the courtyard through the broken front gate which has never been closed in my 3 months of living here, and then actually came inside the building through the broken door to the building and was standing in the hall outside my apartment threatening me with a bottle of alcohol in hand. reviews.author: Mar
reviews.text: Simply avoid this place. Poor infrastructure led to 2 burst pipes in 2 different occasions, months apart. Extreme water damage, no amount of compensation (very poor compensation at that) will save you from being HIGHLY inconvenienced. The fire brigade came by and even then they couldn’t at least stop the water from flo

reviews.author: Shiyao Kuang
reviews.text: All the staff are really helpful and friendly. I moved to BC this month, and the reception helped me move a lot of my belongings into my studio. I moved in urgently, and everything was done in one day. The maintenance team was punctual with my repair requests when I accidentally caused an electrical issue in my studio!

reviews.author: Manu
reviews.text: I believe maintenence is quick and effective. They are always ready to help. Cleanliness is also great, facilities are always clean and tidy. reviews.author: Yordan Gatev
reviews.text: I stayed at The Tannery for a full academic year (2025/26), and overall I would describe it as a reasonably good place to live. reviews.text: I honestly would not recommend anything Yugo. For the amount of rent they charge, the quality is really bad. reviews.author: Ma Am
reviews.text: The rooms are not that clean and perhaps very low ceiling and those on the left side of the entrance gate, no light and facing to rubbish recycling facilities. However, The people who running it at reception though are nice, cooperative and polite people.

reviews.author: Gavin O Keeffe
reviews.text: Lovely clean accommodation and very friendly staff

reviews.author: Molly M
reviews.text: I've lived in Student Village for the last three years, and I absolutely love it. The place is extremely homely and the staff are always so kind and helpful when you have questions. reviews.author: Rocio Espinosa
reviews.text: I have no way to contact them to give me information. Could someone give me an email? The phone number on the website gives an error. They should provide options to contact the current ones, which are scarce and defective. Thank you.

reviews.author: J. P.
reviews.text: Students accommodation this places always welcome because it’s to accommodate students and education always a good investment they come from all parts of world but at moment in cork city I see a lot asiáticos students more than usually. Back to this places was building a few all over town and they are all brand new, to

reviews.author: Conor Yates
reviews.text: Broga House Cork was an excellent choice. Located within walking distance of so much the city has to offer, and easy access to buses that go to the rest of County Cork. The staff were friendly, helpful, and responsive to requests. Would recommend.

reviews.author: Roya
reviews.text: Customer service was questionable from the beginning, though the staff was mostly nice. We stayed there for a weekend, then went for a hike and since it was impossible to store our luggage on site, we had to pack everything and moved it to another hotel. reviews.author: Nooshin Hatami
reviews.text: Honestly, I’ve really enjoyed living here. It’s a great place if you want a balance between studying and relaxing — there are nice study areas, spaces to play games, and even a movie room. The free coffee is a big plus too haha. What I appreciate the most is how understanding and supportive the team is, especially duri

reviews.author: Jane Twomey
reviews.text: The Bottleworks is relatively new, so all the ammenities are too. My studio is lovely, and I never have a problem with maintenance or repair. On the few occasions I have called for repairs, everything was done in a day. I use the app to contact management, and I don't have any issues. I also haven't had any big problem

reviews.author: M F
reviews.text: The accommodation is really great. The staff is also very friendly and helpful if there are any issues. Also great communication from the staff to us to keep us updated and informed on any issues. They also plan a lot of nice activities during the stay. Definitely recommend staying here

name: Room in a 14 Bedroom Apartment, 3 Clonskeagh Road, Rathmines

reviews.author: Ryan Forsythe
reviews.text: Management sucks. They let construction go on until midnight on some nights, as well as starting at 6am sometimes. Constant noise from construction shaking the whole building, as well as a very bright light shining through my window at all hours, even when the establishment below is closed. Management has don’t nothing

reviews.author: Jerry Walker
reviews.text: If I get woken up by drilling that vibrates the entire building one more time, I’m going to chain myself up outside in a hunger strike.

reviews.author: Forest Ruan
reviews.text: Lived there for around 1 year. Good location and it's generally safe. Downsides are, the fire alarm went off way too often, sometimes late at night. "Roasting in summer and freezing in winter" as mentioned in the other review, is absolutely true! The ground floor can be really wet especially in bedroom.

reviews.author: Eri Regis
reviews.text: I had a really positive experience staying here as a student. The staff were consistently friendly and helpful, which made settling in much easier. reviews.author: Mason Miller
reviews.text: Super small rooms, small bed for higher than average price reasoned by the location. The location is not even that great it is similar to many other accommodation on the luas line. No Gym no library and even no tv in the living room.

reviews.author: Milena Boikova
reviews.text: I had a very pleasant stay at The Artisan City Center Hotel in Dublin. The location is excellent, making it easy to explore the city on foot and access all the main attractions. reviews.author: Lavish Thomas
reviews.text: Bit old and outdated building. Not bad for a 2006 apartment. But do not pay rent as much as the modern apartment.

reviews.author: Bake Hole

reviews.author: Merche Benitez
reviews.text: Stella, a wonderful and incredibly attentive employee. We had a problem with the bathroom, she came to look at it, and within minutes they changed our room. She even offered to help us move our belongings to the other room. We need more employees like her! 🤌🏼🤌🏼🤌🏼🤌🏼

reviews.author: Paula Sánchez Rodríguez
